Module 3: Seed Launch, Part 5
Executive Summary
In the last video we talked about the “Hyper” Seed Launch and mentioned
there were two ways to approach it. The first is the “Product Buildout”. The
second is…
#2: “Show Me the Money”
• 	This method is more complicated and harder to pull off - but builds your
list and makes you money as you do it
• 	People have built serious businesses from scratch using this model
• 	It’s possible to mess this up by being overly manipulative - if used
incorrectly it could end up burning bridges and killing the business
• 	In some ways similar to the Product Buildout model - line up experts that
are willing to be interviewed
Each expert gets the opportunity to pitch their product at the end of the
interview (the last 10-15 minutes). The pitch is usually made with an affiliate
link - you get a commission from the sales.
Let the expert know this is part of a series - have around 5 parts/experts.
Use the social proof that other experts will also be on this interview series.
This can backfire if you name drop but haven’t gotten confirmation from the
!
1	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 1 of 3 --

experts you’ve invited yet - be careful here. If you can get 1-2 experts to
say yes it will be easier to get others on board
Important Note: Ask the expert to email their list about the webinar or
teleseminar, then have people join your list to register. As you do this you
build your list with each call.
To get the expert on board use strategies similar to when you’re building
relationships with JV partners. Each time you do an interview email your
own list about the event. Do the call and allow them to pitch at the end.
You could also make it even more attractive by not taking a cut of sales
(this is optional and negotiable). Make sure to follow up after the call with a
replay. Then follow up with an email about the offer - make sure there is a
deadline (scarcity).
The toughest part of this model is getting the first expert to participate and
mail their list. After that, you’re rolling.
• 	You’ll want to use teleseminars and webinar tools for this
• 	You want the calls to be interactive - need ability to mute and unmute
individual lines
• 	For Teleseminars:
• 	Instant Seminar - has muting feature
• 	GoToWebinar - has muting feature
• 	Voice Text - doesn’t have muting feature
!
